channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why Trump dashed Biden’s AI executive order, and why it matters
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first few hours in power, he revoked Joe Biden’s 2023 executive order on AI. In the absence of congressional action, Biden’s EO served as the first effort by the federal government to address the considerable societal and national security risks presented by AI.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Today’s AI models have a poor grasp of world history
item.description: Today’s AI models do a poor job of providing accurate information about world history, according to a new report from the Austrian research institute Complexity Science Hub (CSH).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Samsung and Google's Dolby Atmos rival is coming to Android – but I need it in earbuds before I get excited
item.description: Eclipsa Audio coming to Android is all well and good, but until support for earbuds and headphones lands, I'm on the fence.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order to delay TikTok ban for 75 days
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Monday giving TikTok an additional 75 days before a law banning the popular video-sharing platform takes effect.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Can he do that? Lawyers and lawmakers debate if Trump has authority to suspend the US TikTok ban
item.description: Business leaders, lawmakers, legal scholars, and influencers who make money on TikTok are watching to see how Trump tries to resolve a thicket of regulatory, legal, financial and geopolitical issues with his signature.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Ramaswamy won’t serve on Trump’s government efficiency commission as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the 2024 Republican nomination for president, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Elon Musk's prominence during inauguration ceremonies stamps tech titan's place in Trump's orbit
item.description: The blossoming relationship between President Donald Trump and tech titan Elon Musk was on full display throughout Monday's inauguration ceremonies.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 hot network trends for 2025
item.description: Networking has been a relatively stable industry for decades. On the vendor side of the equation, it’s been Cisco versus everyone else. On the tech side, a slow, steady progression of incrementally faster versions of tried-and-true standards like Wi-Fi and Ethernet was the norm.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lawmakers side-eye billionaire CEOs getting prime Trump inauguration seats
item.description: Billionaire tech CEOs Jeff Bezos of Amazon, Mark Zuckerberg of Meta, Sundar Pichai of Google, Tim Cook of Apple, and Elon Musk got prime seats at President Trump’s inauguration in the Capitol rotunda — peeving lawmakers in both parties.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Jeff Bezos, Lauren Sanchez, Elon Musk and Ivanka Trump partied like oligarchs
item.description: Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner exchanged greetings with Jeff Bezos, Lauren Sanchez and Elon Musk at the candlelight dinner for Donald Trump at the National Building Museum Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 new features in EDB Postgres AI
item.description: More than half of enterprises (56%) run mission-critical workloads in hybrid environments, according to a 2024 EDB survey of enterprise executives.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram announces new video app amid TikTok troubles
item.description: Instagram announced a new video creation app Sunday amid legal struggles faced in the U.S. by TikTok, another social media platform with a focus on visual content.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 is now in a second rampdown phase, with the feature set frozen at 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Army readies new AI guidance based on lessons learned
item.description: The Army is looking at commercial AI use cases that can serve large populations of users, ideally without running up huge bills The post Army readies new AI guidance based on lessons learned first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oracle updates Exadata systems to speed database operations
item.description: If you had forgotten that Oracle was in the hardware business, it’s easy to understand why, as Oracle has not exactly promoted the business very well. Oracle’s hardware is descended from Sun Microsystems servers, which Oracle acquired in 2010.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok ban upheld by Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok and ByteDance — along with some content creators and users — argued the law violates constitutionally protected free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to do if you think your PC has a virus
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals the warning signs and steps to take if you suspect that your personal computer is infected with a virus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Android file transfer: How to manage, move, and sync files
item.description: When you think about your smartphone, apps and interfaces are probably the first things that come to mind. Beneath all that surface-level stuff, though, our modern mobile devices are filled with files — folders upon folders of ’em! — just like the clunky ol’ computers we’ve relied upon for ages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Digital Transformation in Prisons: How Kazakhstan is Leading the Way
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Info World
